vibe: Friday energy — deep house grooves, Japanese fusion sunshine, UK jazz explorations, lo-fi warmth, and math rock precision

tracks.name: Crazy For It
tracks.reason: You've been heavy on &ME lately — Rampa's from the same Keinemusik crew but brings a slightly more melodic, rolling deep house energy that hits different.

tracks.name: BRASILIAN SKIES
tracks.reason: With all the Taeko Onuki and 加藤有紀 you've been spinning, Takanaka's guitar-driven Japanese fusion is the natural next step — pure sunshine boogie from the same era.

tracks.name: Space 2
tracks.reason: Belgian-Caribbean harpist doing cosmic UK jazz — if you dig Floating Points and the ambient side of your listening, this is the next rabbit hole.

tracks.name: Pick Up
tracks.reason: A deep house classic that sits perfectly between the Peggy Gou and &ME in your rotation — hypnotic, warm, and somehow gets better every listen.

tracks.name: Tioga Pass (feat. Rocco Palladino)
tracks.reason: You've got Surprise Chef in your top tracks — Yussef Dayes is the UK jazz drummer who brings that same instrumental groove but with broken beat swagger.

tracks.name: 81 autumn
tracks.reason: Nujabes' right-hand man with a lo-fi jazz instrumental that feels like a Tokyo autumn afternoon — mellow and contemplative without being sleepy.

tracks.name: DEJA VU
tracks.reason: You've been vibing Central Cee's reggaeton collabs — Tainy is the producer behind half the best reggaeton of the last decade, and this one's a slick Friday night starter.

tracks.name: God Gave Me Feet For Dancing (feat. Yazmin Lacey)
tracks.reason: Mercury Prize-winning UK jazz meets afrobeat with Yazmin Lacey's silky vocals — bridges your Sade love and your Surprise Chef instrumental taste.

tracks.name: SPORTS MEN (2018 Yoshinori Sunahara Remastering)
tracks.reason: YMO co-founder doing quirky Japanese electronic — connects your city pop world to the electronic side. Weird, funky, and absolutely brilliant.

tracks.name: グッドバイ -album version-
tracks.reason: Japanese math rock at its most beautiful — intricate guitar work that feels emotional rather than technical. Perfect closer for a Friday playlist.
